status_scale.satisfied: Completion evidence exists and is machine-checkable where practical.
status_scale.partial: Useful repo-side work exists, but the final requirement is not proven.
status_scale.pending_external_evidence: The repo has the harness or process, but real external evidence is still required.
status_scale.deferred_by_user: The requirement is intentionally held until explicit user approval.
status_scale.not_started: No meaningful implementation or validation evidence exists yet.

gates.requirement: Run an API-key-gated OpenAI Responses live smoke and keep only sanitized evidence.
required_evidence: Sanitized live smoke JSON with scenario openai_responses and status passed.
Product-truth register entries for any non-passing OpenAI Responses findings.

current_evidence.proves: The live harness skips safely when OPENAI_API_KEY is absent; it does not prove live provider behavior.
The optional live smoke entrypoint exists.

gates.remaining_action: Run live mode with OPENAI_API_KEY, review sanitized output, and convert any finding into product truth.

gates.requirement: Run an API-key-gated Anthropic prompt-caching live smoke and keep only sanitized evidence.
required_evidence: Sanitized live smoke JSON with scenario anthropic_prompt_caching and status passed.
Product-truth register entries for any non-passing Anthropic caching findings.

current_evidence.proves: The live harness skips safely when ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is absent; it does not prove live provider behavior.
The extractor has fixture-backed cache-token coverage.

gates.remaining_action: Run live mode with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, review sanitized output, and convert any finding into product truth.

gates.requirement: Run a Vercel AI SDK streamText smoke beside a real SDK flow without making the SDK a core dependency.
required_evidence: Sanitized framework smoke JSON with scenario vercel_ai_sdk_stream_text and status passed.
Product-truth register entries for any non-passing Vercel streaming findings.

current_evidence.proves: A dependency-optional Vercel sample/live smoke entrypoint exists.
The streamText finish object shape is fixture-backed.

gates.remaining_action: Run the Vercel live smoke with required SDK dependencies and credentials, then classify any finding.

gates.requirement: Run a LangChain agent or callback smoke beside a real SDK/framework flow.
required_evidence: Sanitized framework smoke JSON with scenario langchain_agent_run and status passed.
Product-truth register entries for any non-passing LangChain findings.

current_evidence.proves: A dependency-optional LangChain sample/live smoke entrypoint exists.
The LangChain callback/context-manager path is fixture-backed.

gates.remaining_action: Run the LangChain live smoke with required framework dependencies and credentials, then classify any finding.

gates.requirement: Run an OpenRouter live smoke and compare RunCost output to provider-reported cost where present.
required_evidence: Sanitized live smoke JSON with scenario openrouter_cost_compare and status passed.
Documented exact, estimated, or mismatch classification for provider-reported cost.

current_evidence.proves: The live harness skips safely when OPENROUTER_API_KEY is absent; it does not prove live provider behavior.
Provider-reported cost modes are fixture-backed.

gates.remaining_action: Run live mode with OPENROUTER_API_KEY and classify any provider-reported cost delta.

gates.requirement: Validate a multi-provider custom-discount case in the alpha smoke harness.
required_evidence: Smoke evidence showing a multi-provider discount case in the same review set as at least one live provider run.
Fixture or documented limitation for any discount behavior discrepancy.

current_evidence.proves: The deterministic no-network sample covers the multi-provider discount scenario.
CI verifies the sample multi-provider discount smoke shape.

gates.remaining_action: Include the discount scenario in the reviewed live smoke report and classify any issues discovered beside live provider evidence.

gates.requirement: Every live smoke finding becomes a fixture, structured warning, documented limitation, extractor/source fix, or price-source update.
required_evidence: Product-truth register entries matching every non-passing item in the reviewed live smoke report.
Referenced artifacts exist for every non-none classification.

current_evidence.proves: The product-truth loop is machine-checkable for current no-credential findings.
Live smoke reports can be checked against the register.

gates.remaining_action: After real live smoke runs, update the register and artifacts for any non-passing findings.

gates.requirement: Compare at least one real provider dashboard, invoice, or usage export against RunCost output.
required_evidence: Sanitized comparison JSON with evidence_type real_provider_export.
Comparison passes the --require-real check and documents exact, estimated, and unsupported fields.

current_evidence.proves: The real-evidence check exists and rejects sanitized samples as completion evidence.
A sanitized sample proves the workflow shape, not real invoice completion.

gates.remaining_action: Run the comparison against a sanitized real provider dashboard, invoice, or usage export.

gates.requirement: Configure PyPI trusted publishing for adamallcock/runcost and the release environment.
required_evidence: Registry-side PyPI trusted-publisher settings are configured for .github/workflows/release.yml and environment release.
A release workflow run reaches the PyPI publish step without requiring a stored API token when publishing is approved.

current_evidence.proves: The required PyPI trusted-publisher settings are documented.
The workflow requests id-token permissions and uses PyPI trusted publishing.

gates.completion_check: Manual registry setting verification plus guarded release workflow evidence.
gates.remaining_action: Configure and verify the PyPI trusted publisher outside the repository.

gates.requirement: Configure npm trusted publishing/provenance for the JavaScript package.
required_evidence: Registry-side npm trusted-publisher settings are configured for release.yml and environment release.
A release workflow run can publish with provenance when publishing is explicitly approved.

current_evidence.proves: The required npm trusted-publisher settings are documented.
The workflow publishes npm with provenance when publishing is enabled.

gates.completion_check: Manual registry setting verification plus guarded release workflow evidence.
gates.remaining_action: Configure and verify npm trusted publishing outside the repository.

gates.requirement: Run the guarded release workflow with publishing disabled for the real intended beta version.
required_evidence: Successful GitHub release workflow run for the intended beta version with publish=false.
Artifact review checklist has been reviewed for that run.

current_evidence.proves: A publish=false rehearsal succeeded for version 0.0.0, but not for the real intended beta version.

gates.completion_check: gh workflow run release.yml --ref main -f version=<real-version> -f publish=false
gates.remaining_action: Select the beta version, run publish=false from main, review artifacts, and record the real-version evidence.

gates.requirement: Verify the Go module from a real semantic version tag without a local replace.
required_evidence: A pushed semantic version tag exists.
Clean external module can run go get github.com/adamallcock/runcost/packages/go/ledger@v<version> without replace.

current_evidence.proves: The real Go tag verification command is documented.
The release workflow verifies the real tag path when the tag exists.

gates.remaining_action: Create/push the real version tag and verify from a clean external module.

gates.requirement: Publish Python and JavaScript packages after explicit artifact review and approval.
required_evidence: Explicit user approval to publish.
PyPI and npm package pages for the released version.
Post-publish install smoke evidence.
gates.completion_check: Guarded release workflow with publish=true after explicit approval.
gates.deferred_reason: The current objective says to leave packages unpublished for now.
gates.remaining_action: Do not publish until the user explicitly approves publication.

gates.requirement: Tighten schemas, source update process, contribution docs, caveats, public support matrix, and dependency/publishing story.
required_evidence: Generated schema docs and taxonomy drift checks pass.
Source-data process, contribution docs, caveats, support matrix, and release docs are current after live smoke findings.

current_evidence.proves: The beta/V1 hardening gates are documented.
A public support matrix exists.
Known caveats are documented.

gates.remaining_action: Refresh public caveats and support docs after live smoke and invoice comparison findings.

gates.requirement: Mature schema-derived types, generated docs tables, drift checks, and Go APIs beyond map-backed prototype paths.
required_evidence: Schema-derived or schema-checked type surfaces exist for the supported languages where practical.
Generated docs and API parity checks cover support matrices, warning codes, and component registries.
Go has stable typed APIs for core user-facing paths.

current_evidence.proves: Python, TypeScript, and Go taxonomy-bearing type artifacts are generated from the locked taxonomy.
Python taxonomy literal aliases are generated and imported by the public type surface.
TypeScript taxonomy literal unions are generated and imported by the public declaration file.
Go taxonomy slices are generated and used by the typed core package.
Generated taxonomy type artifacts and public type surfaces are checked against schemas.
Schema-field reference docs are generated from JSON schemas.
Go typed wrappers exist for core calculation paths.

gates.remaining_action: Keep generated taxonomy type artifacts and public type imports current as schemas evolve.

gates.requirement: Every stable warning code has shared fixture coverage before V1.
required_evidence: Generated warning coverage shows every warning code as fixture-backed.
Shared conformance fixtures cover required metadata for every stable warning code.

current_evidence.proves: Warning-code fixture coverage is generated from taxonomy and fixtures; current output shows all stable warning codes are fixture-backed.
Warning coverage docs are drift-checked with the other generated contract docs.
The unknown_provider warning has shared conformance coverage.
The usage_field_ignored warning has shared conformance coverage.
The inclusive_usage_ambiguous warning has shared conformance coverage.

gates.remaining_action: Keep warning coverage at 100% when warning codes change.

gates.requirement: Broaden real-world coverage for generated media, transcription, rerank, storage/session pricing, stream variants, and framework examples.
required_evidence: Live or fixture-backed provider-specific coverage for prioritized media, transcription, rerank, storage/session, and stream variants.
Smoke-derived framework examples for top integrations.

current_evidence.proves: Normalized feature-pricing components exist for generated media, rerank, and transcription.
Normalized GB-day storage pricing exists.
Framework adapter coverage and remaining limitations are documented.

gates.remaining_action: Use live smoke and provider docs to choose the next provider-specific feature surfaces.

gates.requirement: Stabilize schemas, warning codes, APIs, provider/source coverage, historical pricing, and top framework integrations with no known correctness holes.
required_evidence: Stable schema and warning-code compatibility policy.
Production-ready Python and JavaScript packages and stable Go core APIs.
Historical-pricing path and strong coverage for every surface marked supported.
No known correctness holes in supported surfaces.
gates.remaining_action: Finish Milestone 8 and public beta first, then promote stable APIs and contracts toward V1.
